key_insight: A multimodal pretreatment biomarker signature modestly improved prediction of primary resistance to anti-PD-(L)1 therapy in advanced NSCLC, outperforming PD-L1 and TMB alone.
clinical_relevance: This suggests that combining clinical, blood-based, tumor, and molecular features may better stratify patients before immunotherapy than any single biomarker.
method: Prospective, multicenter PIONeeR study in 439 patients across 17 centers.
Six-layer pretreatment profiling: clinical data, routine labs, circulating immune/soluble markers, digital pathology, transcriptomics, and genomics.
Compared 36 feature-selection methods and 10 machine-learning models under .632 optimism correction.
evidence_signal: The 18-feature multimodal model achieved optimism-corrected AUROC 0.73±0.05 and PPV 0.63±0.07 overall, versus PPV 0.36 for PD-L1 and 0.35 for TMB.
watch_item: The signature is promising but still needs external validation and practical standardization before routine clinical implementation.
paper_title: Multimodal profiling for prediction of primary resistance to anti-PD-(L)1 therapy in advanced non-small-cell lung cancer: the prospective PIONeeR biomarkers study
